 {"id":"bd-pu3k","title":"Implement single-level timer wheel","description":"## Overview

Implement a basic single-level timer wheel as the foundation for the hierarchical version.

## Background

Start simple with a single wheel, then extend to hierarchical. This allows validating the core mechanics before adding complexity.

## Design

### Wheel Structure
```rust
pub struct TimerWheel<const SLOTS: usize> {
    /// Circular array of timer slots
    slots: [TimerSlot; SLOTS],
    /// Current position in the wheel
    current: usize,
    /// Resolution in ticks (e.g., 1ms)
    resolution: Duration,
    /// Timers pending in the wheel
    count: usize,
}

struct TimerSlot {
    /// Head of intrusive linked list
    head: Option<NonNull<TimerNode>>,
}
```

### Timer Node (Intrusive)
```rust
pub struct TimerNode {
    /// Links for intrusive list
    next: Option<NonNull<TimerNode>>,
    prev: Option<NonNull<TimerNode>>,
    /// Waker to call on expiration
    waker: Option<Waker>,
    /// Slot this timer is in (for O(1) cancel)
    slot: usize,
    /// Absolute expiration time
    deadline: Instant,
}
```

### Operations
```rust
impl<const SLOTS: usize> TimerWheel<SLOTS> {
    pub fn insert(&mut self, node: &mut TimerNode, deadline: Instant);
    pub fn cancel(&mut self, node: &mut TimerNode);
    pub fn tick(&mut self) -> Vec<Waker>;  // Returns expired wakers
    pub fn next_expiration(&self) -> Option<Duration>;
}
```

## Implementation Notes

- Use intrusive lists to avoid allocation
- TimerNode is pinned (cannot move while in wheel)
- Slot index = (deadline / resolution) % SLOTS
- Tick advances current, processes slot

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Single wheel with configurable slot count
- [ ] O(1) insert via slot hashing
- [ ] O(1) cancel via list removal
- [ ] O(1) tick processing (amortized)
- [ ] Unit tests for basic operations
- [ ] Handles wrap-around correctly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyOtter","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T18:02:39.751998165Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-20T23:54:09.966499884Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T23:54:09.965587526Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-q0om","title":"[Harmonize] Merge I/O EPICs: 90l9 → ds8","description":"# [Harmonize] Merge I/O EPICs: 90l9 → ds8

## Purpose
Migrate all unique sub-EPICs from asupersync-90l9 into asupersync-ds8 hierarchy, then close 90l9.

## Sub-EPICs to Migrate
- wx8h: Core Reactor
- l92b: epoll
- 3z8z: kqueue  
- 7tk3: Lab Reactor
- ui2r: TCP/UDP
- ycir: UDS
- 56fs: Integration Testing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 90l9 sub-EPICs linked to ds8 hierarchy via dependencies
- [ ] 90l9 closed with migration note referencing ds8
- [ ] No orphaned sub-EPICs after merge
- [ ] ds8 description updated to reflect absorbed scope
- [ ] `br show asupersync-ds8` shows correct dependent tree

## Verification
```bash
br show asupersync-90l9  # Should be closed
br show asupersync-ds8   # Should show all I/O sub-EPICs
bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'  # No cycles
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ClaudeOpus45","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:28:16.599858279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:23:32.548968635Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:23:32.548896109Z","close_reason":"Verified: 90l9 already merged into ds8 hierarchy.  {"id":"bd-q48","title":"[EPIC] Cancel-Aware Synchronization Primitives (tokio-sync equivalent)","description":"# Cancel-Aware Synchronization Primitives

## Overview
Synchronization primitives that integrate with cancellation protocol and support two-phase patterns where needed.

## Components

### 1. Mutex
- lock() returns LockGuard (obligation)
- try_lock() for non-blocking
- Cancel-aware: waiting for lock respects cancellation
- Unlock is automatic via Drop

### 2. RwLock
- read() returns ReadGuard
- write() returns WriteGuard
- Upgradable read locks
- Cancel-aware waiting

### 3. Semaphore
- Two-phase: acquire_permit() returns Permit obligation
- Permit must be released or explicitly dropped
- Bounded and unbounded variants
- OwnedPermit for 'static lifetime

### 4. Barrier
- wait() blocks until N waiters
- Returns BarrierWaitResult (is_leader)
- Cancel-aware: if cancelled, barrier may never complete

### 5. Notify
- notify_one(), notify_waiters()
- notified() returns future
- Cancel-aware waiting

### 6. OnceCell
- get_or_init() with async initializer
- get_or_try_init() for fallible init
- Cancel-aware: racing initializers handled correctly

### 7. Watch Channel
- Single-producer, multi-consumer
- Receivers get latest value
- Cancel-aware subscription

## Invariants
- No deadlocks in well-formed programs
- Cancel-safe acquisition
- Obligations properly tracked
- Deterministic behavior in lab runtime

## Testing
- Concurrent access patterns
- Cancellation during wait
- Deadlock detection (debug builds)
- Deterministic scheduling in lab

 {"id":"bd-qbcnu","title":"[SPORK] Crash Artifacts: Minimal Repro Packs + Canonical Traces","description":"On failures, emit deterministic crash packs (seed + minimal divergent prefix + normalized canonical trace + optional TLA export) so concurrency bugs become reproducible artifacts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:58:16.330341483Z","created_by":"NavyBeacon","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:58:16.352477008Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","replay","spork","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qbcnu","depends_on_id":"bd-1jsq9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:58:16.352434248Z","created_by":"NavyBeacon"}]}
